svg.text: prepare_context(source_path, cfg) → Context(aa, chains, roles, config)
run QC units (pdb_quality_control) → flag bad structures
_plan_plugin_execution(specs) → ordered list of plugin specs to run
read _prepared/pdb + each _plugins/{name}/pdb → LEFT JOIN on identity keys
runs dataset_calculation plugins: CDR entropy, interface summary, annotations…
② Chunked Run run_chunked_pipeline(cfg, chunk_size, workers)
validate merge_compatibility across chunks → STACK pdb frames → concat status + bad
reads fully merged pdb.parquet → dataset_calculation plugins → dataset.parquet
cannot run per-chunk: needs complete population for entropy, summaries, etc.
③ Planned Run plan + run_pipeline + merge
for each chunk: prepare_chunk_input_dir() + write chunk_N/config.yaml
for each chunk: minimum-atw run --config chunk_N/config.yaml
same as other modes — always runs on fully merged table
triggered by should_run_post_merge_dataset_analyses() on source Config
Flexibility: What Can Run Independently, Incrementally, or Out of Order?
Once prepared structures exist in _prepared/, you can call run_plugin() / run_plugins()
repeatedly without re-preparing. Add new plugins to cfg and re-run only the new ones.
Crash during prepare? Resume from last completed structure. Same for plugin execution.
Plugins run sequentially in configured order. Each writes to its own
_plugins/<name>/pdb.parquet, then all are merged in one final pass.
minimum-atw execute → only runs run_plugins() + merge_outputs()
Merge is always the last PDB step
merge_outputs() performs LEFT JOIN: base pdb ← plugin_1 ← plugin_2 ← …
merge_dataset_outputs() performs STACK: combines same-schema outputs from chunks.
These are two distinct operations — LEFT JOIN ≠ STACK.
Dataset Analysis: When & Why Only After Full Merge
Q: Can I run dataset analysis before or during merge?
No. analyze_dataset_outputs() reads the complete merged pdb.parquet.
Dataset-level statistics (entropy, interface summaries, annotations) require the
full population of structures to produce meaningful results.
Q: Can I run dataset analysis on each chunk and then merge?
No — dataset.parquet rows are not simply stackable (e.g. entropy per-chunk ≠ entropy
over full dataset). Always merge first, then analyze once on merged output.
Q: Can I re-run dataset analysis with different params without re-running pipeline?
It only reads the existing merged pdb.parquet — no structure re-processing.
Q: When does analyze_dataset_outputs run automatically?
Triggered at end of run_pipeline(), run_chunked_pipeline(), merge_planned_chunks().
cfg.dataset_annotations: dict mapping structure path → metadata columns
Merged into dataset.parquet. Does not affect pdb.parquet.
grain=role: path, assembly_id, role grain=interface: path, assembly_id, pair
Every plugin declares name and prefix. All output columns: {prefix}__{col}
Enforces no collision between plugins at registry validation time.
prefix_row(prefix, row_dict) wraps yield dict before writing.
BufferedMode: accumulates rows in memory, spills to disk when threshold hit.
Checkpointed mode: append parquet after each structure instead.
merge_table_frames: LEFT JOIN — adds plugin columns alongside base rows.
stack_table_frames: UNION — concatenates disjoint-path chunks row-wise.
Config.merge_compatibility() produces a fingerprint (roles, interface_pairs, assembly_id,
plugins). merge_dataset_outputs() rejects sources with mismatched fingerprints.
minimum_atomworks runtime — Prepare is cached · Execute is incremental · Merge is deterministic · Dataset analysis always runs post-merge
